DEMURRER TO THE ENTIRE COMPLAINT BY LEE AND BERKELEY ROOF SERVICES, INC IS OVERRULED. ALLEGATIONS IN THE COMPLAINT MUST BE TAKEN AS TRUE AT THE PLEADING STAGE. DEMURRER TO THE CAUSE OF ACTION FOR PREMISES LIABILITY IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND FOR PLAINTIFF TO ALLEGE FACTS SUPPORTING THIS CAUSE OF ACTION AS TO EACH DEFENDANT SHOWING HOW EACH DEFENDANT WAS IN POSSESSION AND CONTROL OF THE PREMISES/OR PART THEREOF ON APRIL 18, 2022. DEMURRER TO THE CAUSE OF ACTION FOR IIED ASSERTED BY CORPORATE PLAINTIFFS IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. NO OPPOSITION FILED. DEMURRER TO THE CAUSE OF ACTION FOR IIED BY OTHER PLAINTIFFS IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND FOR EACH PLAINTIFF TO ALLEGE FACTS IN SUPPORT OF EACH ELEMENT OF THIS CAUSE OF ACTION TO EACH DEFENDANT. PLAINTIFF SHALL NOT MAKE ANY GENERALIZED ALLEGATIONS AS TO ALL "ROOFING DEFENDANTS" AND SHALL MAKE SPECIFIC ALLEGATIONS AS TO EACH DEFENDANT. TO THE EXTENT PLAINTIFF RELIED ON ALTER EGO LIABILITY PLAINTIFF MUST ALLEGE ALL ELEMENTS OF ALTER EGO WITH REQUISITE SPECIFICITY. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-17-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS WESTCO ROOFING COMPANY, INC., BERKELEY ROOF SERVICES, INC., ROOFERS SUPPLY CO, INC., AND TAE SON LEE'S AMENDED MOTION TO STRIKE PLAINTIFFS' SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT

No appearances. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: DENIED. The allegation that the "fire was ... determined to have been caused by ROOFING Defendants" is not false or misleading because it does not necessarily imply a judicial determination of causation. The punitive damages allegations and prayers should not be stricken because there are sufficient facts that Defendants acted with intent, malice, recklessness, and/or fraudulent intent given the allegations of prior fires that Defendants caused with blow torch methods, the dangers of using blow torch methods, and prior fraud by Defendants. Additionally, claims that the use of a blow torch is lawful is irrelevant on a motion to strike. Finally, allegations about Mr. Lee's past criminal conduct are relevant and not impermissible character evidence because the allegations support a theory of negligent hiring and punitive damages. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Charles F. Haines, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (501/CFH)

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-17-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS WESTCO ROOFING COMPANY, INC., BERKELEY ROOF SERVICES, INC., ROOFERS SUPPLY CO., INC., AND TAE SON LEE'S AMENDED DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT

No appearances. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: OVERRULED. As to Defendant Berkeley Roofing Services demurrer, the Court's prior order forbids lumping together Defendants in the IIED cause of action. Additionally, the fact that only one Defendant had a permit does not mean all Defendants were not on the roof. As to Defendant Mr. Lee's demurrer, the SAC alleges sufficient facts to support an alter ego theory. The SAC newly alleges, based on Mr. Lee's prior deposition testimony, that the corporate Defendants are a joint venture with the same employees and that these Defendants have "never had any other customers" than each other. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Charles F. Haines, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (501/CFH)
